title: Missouri is not Abortion-Free. But Thanks to Sen. Mike Moon, it Can Be.
author: James Silberman
orientation: Silberman pushes back on pro-life claims that Missouri became 'abortion-free' in December 2020, arguing the state still legally permits abortion in multiple forms, and points to Sen. Mike Moon's SB391 (the Abolition of Abortion in Missouri Act) as the only measure that would actually end it.
audiences: Pro-life advocate skeptical of abolitionist critique

questions.question: Why does the author dispute the claim that Missouri is 'abortion-free' as of December 2020?
questions.audience: Pro-life advocate skeptical of abolitionist critique
questions.answer: Because the claim rests only on the fact that the lone surgical facility in St. Louis paused procedures for one month and referred women across state lines, while abortion itself remains fully legal in Missouri through hospitals, OBGYN offices, and abortifacient drugs.
questions.quote: Murdering a child is still perfectly legal in the state. Babies can be torn to shreds at the St. Louis facility. They can still be murdered at hospitals and OBGYN offices. They can still be murdered by abortifacient drugs.

questions.question: What did the St. Louis facility actually do in December that prompted the 'abortion-free' headlines?
questions.answer: It did not perform surgical abortions for the month and instead routed mothers to a facility roughly 22 minutes away across the Illinois border, where the killings continued.
questions.quote: Instead the St. Louis facility referred mothers to a facility 22 minutes away across the Illinois border.

questions.question: What does SB391 actually do, in legal terms?
questions.answer: It redefines 'person' to include human beings from conception and instructs Missouri to enforce that protection regardless of any contrary federal statute, ruling, or court order.
questions.quote: The bill establishes that “‘Person’, includes a human being from the moment of conception,” and that “This chapter shall be enforced in relation to abortion regardless of any contrary or conflicting federal acts, laws, treaties, decisions, orders, rules, or regulations. Any court decision purporting to enjoin this state from protecting innocent human life from the moment of conception shall be treated as nonauthoritative, void, and of no force.”

questions.question: Why does the author single out point three of the previously passed pro-life bill as a contradiction?
questions.answer: Because after declaring Missouri a 'sanctuary of life' that defends all humans born and unborn, the same bill turned around and limited the state's intent to merely regulating abortion within whatever the federal government and Supreme Court allowed.
questions.quote: Defending the right to life of all humans and making the state a “sanctuary of life” would seem to indicate that the bill intended to abolish abortion. But then in point three, the bill contradicts itself by inexplicably stating that:

questions.question: How exactly does Moon's bill rewrite that contradictory third point?
questions.answer: It strikes 'regulate' and the federal-deference language and replaces it with a direct command to abolish abortion in the state.
questions.quote: Moon’s bill amends point three to read, “[it is the intention of the general assembly of the state of Missouri to] ~~[r]egulate~~ abolish abortion ~~to the full extent permitted by the Constitution of the United States, decisions of the United States Supreme Court, and federal statutes~~ in this state.”

questions.question: Why does the author think prematurely declaring victory in Missouri is harmful, not just inaccurate?
questions.answer: Because real legislative efforts are underway to actually abolish abortion, and announcing the battle is already won undercuts the urgency and momentum needed to pass them.
questions.quote: Given the existence of efforts to actually abolish abortion in the state, claiming the battle is already won is extremely unhelpful.

questions.question: What is Mike Moon's legislative history on this issue?
questions.answer: He served in the Missouri State House from 2013 until terming out, authored the Abolition of Abortion in Missouri Act there in 2020, and is now carrying it in his first State Senate session.
questions.quote: The 2021 session will be Moon’s first year in the State Senate after terming out of the State House in which he had served since 2013. Moon also authored The Abolition of Abortion in Missouri Act in the State House in 2020.

questions.question: What concrete next step does the article ask Missouri abolitionists to take for SB391?
questions.answer: Once the bill receives a committee assignment, supporters need to pressure that committee's chair directly to grant it a hearing and a vote.
questions.quote: SB391 now awaits a committee assignment. When it does, Missourians will need to be in contact with the chair of the committee demanding a hearing and vote for the bill.

related.title: Missouri Abolition Bill Author Mike Moon Elected to State Senate
related.reason: Direct prequel covering Moon's election to the Senate seat from which he files SB391 in this article.

related.reason: Later Missouri-specific account of institutional resistance to the same abolition push.

related.title: June V. Russo Must Be the Death Knell of Pro-Life Judicial Supremacy and Incrementalism
related.reason: Develops the underlying critique of regulating abortion 'to the full extent permitted' by federal courts that Moon's bill rejects.

related.reason: Explains the immediatist framework that motivates Silberman's objection to celebrating partial wins.
